title: Seafood soup
excerpt: Ingredients 1 x 405 g tin concentrated cream of tomato soup 1 x 400 g tin pea soup 1 x 385 g tin lite evaporated milk 250 ml prepared fish or chicken stock 1 x 68 g packet Gourmet Seafood a'la Crème soup powder 1.2 kg mixed fish: equal parts skinned frozen hake cubes, frozen calamari rings …
html:
Ingredients

  • 1 x 405 g tin concentrated cream of tomato soup
  • 1 x 400 g tin pea soup
  • 1 x 385 g tin lite evaporated milk
  • 250 ml prepared fish or chicken stock
  • 1 x 68 g packet Gourmet Seafood a'la Crème soup powder
  • 1.2 kg mixed fish: equal parts skinned frozen hake cubes, frozen calamari rings and blanched mussels in half shells
  • 125 – 200 ml cream
  • 65 ml chopped garlic or plain chives (chives)

Method

Put the cream of tomato soup, pea soup, evaporated milk and stock in a large saucepan and bring to the boil, stirring the mixture occasionally.

Mix the soup powder with a little cold water until smooth and add it to the soup.

Whisk with a wire whisk until it has thickened.

Add the hake, put a lid on and cook on medium for 2 minutes.

Stir in the calamari and mussels, cover again and cook for a further 2 minutes

Be careful: the soup burns very easily, so keep the heat moderate, stir gently every so often, and rather undercook than overcook.

Now remove the soup from the heat and let it stand for at least another 2 minutes to complete the cooking of the fish. Then stir in enough cream to taste and serve.

Makes 8 servings with lovely big, crispy cheese croûtons.

To jazz it up: You can add finely chopped garlic and green chillies to taste, and of course, depending on whether or not you use chillies, season the soup further to taste with finely chopped basil or coriander leaves.

Easy!
